questions.prompt: Transform to passive: "They make these shoes in Italy." The correct passive is:

options.text: These shoes are made in Italy.
These shoes were made in Italy.
These shoes is made in Italy.
These shoes are make in Italy.

questions.explanation: The active verb "make" is present simple, so the passive stays present. "These shoes" is plural → ARE + the past participle "made": these shoes are made in Italy. "were made" changes the tense to past; "is made" wrongly uses the singular "is"; "are make" keeps the base verb instead of the participle.

questions.prompt: Transform to passive: "The teacher corrected our homework." The correct passive is:

options.text: Our homework is corrected by the teacher.
Our homework were corrected by the teacher.
Our homework was correct by the teacher.
Our homework was corrected by the teacher.

questions.explanation: The active verb "corrected" is past simple, and "our homework" is singular (uncountable), so the passive is WAS + the past participle "corrected": our homework was corrected by the teacher. "is corrected" changes the tense; "were corrected" wrongly uses the plural "were"; "was correct" uses the adjective/base form, not the past participle.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence where the passive is used correctly:

options.text: The pyramids were build thousands of years ago.
The pyramids were built thousands of years ago.
The pyramids was built thousands of years ago.
The pyramids are built thousands of years ago.

questions.explanation: "The pyramids" is plural and "thousands of years ago" is past, so we use WERE + the past participle "built": the pyramids were built. "were build" keeps the base verb instead of the participle; "was built" wrongly uses the singular "was"; "are built" is present and clashes with the past time marker.

questions.prompt: Active: "A student finds a wallet." → Passive:

options.text: A wallet is found by a student.
A wallet found by a student.
A wallet were found by a student.
A wallet was found by a student.

questions.explanation: The active verb "finds" is present simple, and "a wallet" is singular, so the passive is IS + the past participle "found": a wallet is found by a student. Option b drops the auxiliary "is"; "were found" wrongly uses the plural past "were"; "was found" changes the tense to past.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly OMITS the by-agent because it is obvious:

options.text: The patient was examined by a doctor.
The patient is examining.
The patient was examined.
The patient examines.

questions.explanation: When it is obvious that a doctor performs the examination, "by a doctor" adds no information and is correctly omitted: The patient was examined.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence with the correct passive: "The new library ___ last year." (open)

options.text: is opened
was opened
opened
were opened

questions.explanation: "last year" signals past tense and "the new library" is singular, so the passive is WAS + the past participle "opened": the new library was opened last year. "is opened" is present; "opened" alone is the active past, not the passive; "were opened" wrongly uses the plural "were".
